title: CSS Best Practices Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on writing maintainable, scalable, and bug-free CSS.

questions.question: Which of these is a recommended naming convention for CSS classes?
options: Style-based (.red-text)
Meaningful/Semantic (.alert-error)
Single characters (.a, .b, .c)
Using only IDs (#main-alert)

questions.question: Why should you generally avoid using IDs for styling in CSS?
options: They have extremely low specificity.
They are only for JavaScript.
They have very high specificity and are hard to override.
They are not supported in older browsers.

questions.question: What does 'box-sizing: border-box' ensure?
options: Borders are removed from all elements.
Padding and borders are included in the element's width and height.
The border is always 1px solid black.
Elements are always centered on the page.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a 'CSS Reset'?
options: To clear all user-defined styles.
To create a consistent baseline of default styles across all browsers.
To minify the CSS file automatically.
To add vendor prefixes to all properties.

questions.question: When is it appropriate to use the '!important' rule?
options: To override any style that isn't working.
In every single declaration to ensure it's applied correctly.
As a last resort for specific utility overrides or complex bug fixes.
To increase the performance of the rendering engine.
